content.text:
1	# Task Prompt
2	
3	Implement a Hospitality Operations Intelligence & Group Itinerary API for offline hotel groups and property management teams to coordinate guest movement plans while producing operational reporting from locally stored data. The backend, built with Express using Sequelize over MySQL, exposes Authentication and Account APIs that support only local username + password login (minimum 10 characters, at least 1 number and 1 symbol), role-based authorization, profile management (legal name, US mailing address, tax invoice title, preferred currency display), and self-service account deletion plus a data export entry point that packages the user’s profile, activity logs, and uploaded files into a locally generated archive. Core collaboration is provided through Itinerary Group APIs that support creating groups, inviting members by pre-shared join code, and maintaining structured itinerary items such as meetup time (MM/DD/YYYY, 12-hour time), meetup location text, route checkpoints (ordered, max 30), and notes (max 2,000 chars). Groups can configure required member data fields (e.g., vehicle make/model/license plate, emergency contact name and US phone format) with per-field required/optional flags; missing required fields block check-in to a meetup. File/attachment APIs support image/document upload up to 10 MB per file with MIME-type allowlists and SHA-256 fingerprinting for deduplication; access control is enforced so only group members can read, while only owners/admins can delete. Change notifications are implemented as in-system event records (no email/SMS/push) that members can query by cursor, including what changed, who changed it, and when, with idempotency keys to prevent duplicate updates during retries.
4	
5	The data query and reporting center provides Reporting and Export APIs that support configurable filters and aggregations over reservations and room inventory, including occupancy rate, ADR, RevPAR, revenue mix by channel, and rollups by day/week/month, room type, and channel. Roles are Hotel Administrator (full access), Manager (property-scoped access), Analyst (read-only reporting), and Member (itinerary-only). Permission rules constrain visibility to assigned properties and prevent export of PII unless explicitly granted; exported CSV/Excel files are generated locally and logged with who/when/filters used. Data import APIs accept template-based Excel for staffing and evaluation datasets with strict column validation, duplicate merge rules (match on employee_id + effective_date), and an error receipt artifact listing row number, field, and reason; reports include position distribution, staffing gaps, evaluation results, and rewards/penalties with signed-off-by metadata. Face enrollment APIs support guided capture metadata (multi-angle required: left/front/right) and local liveness checks based on blink timing, motion consistency, and texture/reflection heuristics; only encrypted face templates are stored (AES-256 at rest) with versioning, update, and deactivation, and raw images are optional with default retention of 24 hours then hard-delete. Data quality and observability requirements include integrity checks (null coverage thresholds, duplication ratios, and outlier detection using configurable z-score bounds), end-to-end trace IDs across ingestion, reporting jobs, and exports, plus operational metrics for job duration, queue depth, and DB resource usage. Automatic retry uses exponential backoff capped at 3 attempts, and rollback is enforced via transactional boundaries for imports and itinerary edits; the entire system must run on a single offline Docker host with no external dependencies, with audit logs immutable for 1 year and sensitive fields masked in logs by default.
6	 
7

1	# Hospitality Operations Intelligence — Feature Overview
2	
3	Offline-first backend API platform for hotel group itinerary coordination, operational reporting, staffing imports, and face enrollment. Built with Express (TypeScript) + Sequelize + MySQL. No UI. Pure backend.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Authentication and Accounts
8	
9	What it does: Local username/password login with JWT, profile management, and self-service account deletion.
10	
11	What needs to be built:
12	- Registration with password policy: min 10 chars, at least 1 number, 1 symbol
13	- Login endpoint with bcrypt verification and JWT issuance
14	- Account lockout after repeated failed attempts
15	- Profile management: legal name, US mailing address, tax invoice title, preferred currency
16	- Password change endpoint
17	- Self-service account deletion (soft delete)
18	- Data export endpoint: packages profile, activity logs, and uploaded files into a local ZIP archive
19	- Audit log on login, logout, profile change, deletion
20	
21	---
22	
23	## Role-Based Authorization
24	
25	What it does: Four-role RBAC with property-scoped access for managers.
26	
27	What needs to be built:
28	- Role enum: hotel_admin (full access), manager (property-scoped), analyst (read-only reporting), member (itinerary-only)
29	- JWT claims include role and property_id
30	- RBAC middleware enforces role on every protected route
31	- Manager queries always filtered by assigned property_id
32	- PII export requires explicit permission grant (not default for any role)
33	- Wrong role → 403
34	
35	---
36	
37	## Itinerary Groups
38	
39	What it does: Create and manage travel/coordination groups with join codes and member management.
40	
41	What needs to be built:
42	- Group CRUD (name, status)
43	- Join code generation (unique, pre-shared)
44	- Join group by code endpoint
45	- Member list and role management (owner, admin, member)
46	- Remove member endpoint (owner/admin only)
47	- Archive group endpoint
48	- Configure required member data fields per group (field name, type, required/optional flag)
49	- Member field value submission (vehicle make/model/license plate, emergency contact, etc.)
50	- US phone format validation for phone-type fields
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Itinerary Items
55	
56	What it does: Structured itinerary items with checkpoints, notes, and member check-in.
57	
58	What needs to be built:
59	- Itinerary item CRUD within a group
60	- Meetup date in MM/DD/YYYY format
61	- Meetup time in 12-hour format (HH:MM AM/PM)
62	- Meetup location text field
63	- Route checkpoints: ordered list, max 30 per item
64	- Notes field: max 2,000 characters
65	- Idempotency key required on create/update to prevent duplicate updates during retries
66	- Check-in endpoint: validates all required member fields are filled before allowing check-in
67	- Missing required fields → 400 with list of missing fields
68	
69	---
70	
71	## File and Attachment Management
72	
73	What it does: Upload and manage files with deduplication and group-scoped access control.
74	
75	What needs to be built:
76	- File upload endpoint (multipart/form-data), max 10 MB per file
77	- MIME-type allowlist: images (jpeg, png, gif, webp) and documents (pdf, docx, xlsx)
78	- SHA-256 fingerprinting for deduplication (return existing file if hash matches)
79	- Associate files with groups
80	- Access control: only group members can read files; only owner/admin can delete
81	- File access log (who read/deleted, when)
82	- File list endpoint (group-scoped)
83	
84	---
85	
86	## Change Notifications
87	
88	What it does: In-system event records for group changes, queryable by cursor.
89	
90	What needs to be built:
91	- Emit notification events on: item created/updated/deleted, member joined/removed, file uploaded/deleted, required field config changed
92	- Notification record: event_type, actor, resource_type, resource_id, detail (what changed), timestamp
93	- Cursor-based query endpoint: `GET /notifications?groupId=&after=<cursor>&limit=`
94	- Idempotency key on notification creation (duplicate key = silent ignore)
95	- Mark notification as read per user
96	
97	---
98	
99	## Reporting and Analytics
100	
101	What it does: Configurable reporting over reservations and room inventory with CSV/Excel export.
102	
103	What needs to be built:
104	- Occupancy rate: occupied rooms / total rooms per period
105	- ADR (Average Daily Rate): total room revenue / occupied room nights
106	- RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room): total revenue / total available room nights
107	- Revenue mix by channel (direct, OTA, corporate, group)
108	- Rollups by day, week, month
109	- Rollups by room type
110	- Rollups by channel
111	- Configurable filters: property, date range, room type, channel, status
112	- CSV and Excel export of report results
113	- Export log: who exported, when, which filters used
114	- Property-scoped access for managers (cannot see other properties)
115	- PII fields excluded from export unless explicit permission granted
116	
117	---
118	
119	## Data Import — Staffing and Evaluations
120	
121	What it does: Template-based Excel import for staffing and evaluation datasets.
122	
123	What needs to be built:
124	- Excel template download per dataset type (staffing, evaluation)
125	- Upload and validate endpoint: strict column validation, returns error receipt artifact
126	- Error receipt: row number, field name, reason for each error
127	- Duplicate merge rule: match on employee_id + effective_date → update existing record
128	- Staffing report: position distribution, staffing gaps per property
129	- Evaluation report: results summary, rewards/penalties, signed-off-by metadata
130	- Retry failed imports: exponential backoff, max 3 attempts
131	- Transactional import: rollback entire batch on unrecoverable error
132	
133	---
134	
135	## Face Enrollment
136	
137	What it does: Guided multi-angle face capture with local liveness checks and encrypted template storage.
138	
139	What needs to be built:
140	- Enrollment session start endpoint
141	- Capture endpoint per angle (left, front, right) — all 3 required
142	- Liveness check per capture: blink timing, motion consistency, texture/reflection heuristics
143	- Encrypted face template generation (AES-256 at rest)
144	- Template versioning (new enrollment creates new version, old deactivated)
145	- Template update and deactivation endpoints
146	- Raw image optional storage with 24-hour hard-delete
147	- Face template list per user (versions, status)
148	
149	---
150	
151	## Data Quality and Observability
152	
153	What it does: Configurable integrity checks, trace IDs, and operational metrics.
154	
155	What needs to be built:
156	- Integrity check rules: null coverage thresholds, duplication ratios, outlier detection (configurable z-score bounds)
157	- Run checks on demand and on schedule (hourly)
158	- Quality check results stored with pass/fail and trace ID
159	- End-to-end trace IDs on all ingestion, reporting, and export operations
160	- Operational metrics: job duration, queue depth, DB resource usage (stored in DB)
161	- Structured JSON logging with trace ID on every request
162	
163	---
164	
165	## Security and Audit
166	
167	What it does: Encryption, masking, immutable audit trail, transactional safety.
168	
169	What needs to be built:
170	- bcrypt password hashing (rounds=12)
171	- AES-256-GCM for face templates and sensitive fields
172	- Sensitive fields masked in all log output
173	- Append-only audit_logs (no DELETE for app DB role), 1-year retention
174	- Transactional boundaries for imports and itinerary edits (rollback on failure)
175	- Exponential backoff retry (max 3 attempts) for import jobs
176	- Trace IDs on every request (X-Trace-Id header)
177

content.text:
1	# Hospitality Operations Intelligence — Feature Build Order
2	
3	Build one slice at a time. Each slice must be fully working (implementation + tests) before moving to the next.
4	
5	Acceptance execution rule for every slice:
6	- Capture objective evidence (endpoint result, test output, log sample, migration output).
7	- Record evidence locations as `path:line` in the acceptance report.
8	- If execution is blocked by environment limits, document exact command + boundary of what is confirmable.
9	
10	---
11	
12	## Slice 1 — Project Foundation
13	Done when:
14	- Express app boots with Sequelize connected to MySQL
15	- `docker compose up` starts cleanly
16	- Sequelize migrations run on startup
17	- Structured logging with trace IDs on every request (X-Trace-Id header)
18	- Log categories exist and are consistent (`auth`, `rbac`, `audit`, `import`, `reporting`, `security`, `system`)
19	- Health endpoint `GET /health` returns 200
20	- `.env.example` has all required vars
21	- `run_tests.sh` runs unit + integration tests
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Slice 2 — Authentication and Accounts
26	Done when:
27	- `POST /auth/register` creates user with bcrypt password (min 10 chars, 1 number, 1 symbol enforced)
28	- `POST /auth/login` validates credentials, returns JWT
29	- `POST /auth/logout` invalidates session
30	- `PATCH /auth/change-password` enforces password policy
31	- Account lockout after repeated failed attempts
32	- `GET /accounts/me` returns profile
33	- `PATCH /accounts/me` updates legal name, US address, tax invoice title, preferred currency
34	- `POST /accounts/me/delete` soft-deletes account after password confirmation
35	- `POST /accounts/me/export` packages profile + activity logs + file list into local ZIP
36	- Audit log on login, logout, profile change, deletion
37	- Unit tests: password policy, bcrypt, JWT
38	- Integration tests: register, login, wrong password, lockout, profile update, export
39	
40	---
41	
42	## Slice 3 — RBAC
43	Done when:
44	- Role enum enforced: hotel_admin, manager, analyst, member
45	- JWT claims include role and property_id
46	- RBAC middleware rejects wrong role with 403
47	- Manager queries filtered by property_id
48	- PII export blocked unless explicit permission granted
49	- Route-level authorization tests exist (401/403 on protected routes)
50	- Object-level authorization tests exist (cannot access/modify resources by ID without membership/ownership)
51	- Data isolation tests exist (cross-group and cross-property denial)
52	- Integration tests: role enforcement, cross-property 403, member-only itinerary access
53	
54	---
55	
56	## Slice 4 — Itinerary Groups
57	Done when:
58	- `POST /groups` creates group with unique join code
59	- `POST /groups/join` joins group by code
60	- `GET /groups` lists own groups
61	- `GET/PATCH /groups/:id` get and update group
62	- `GET /groups/:id/members` lists members
63	- `DELETE /groups/:id/members/:userId` removes member (owner/admin only)
64	- `GET/POST /groups/:id/required-fields` manage required field configs
65	- `PATCH/DELETE /groups/:id/required-fields/:fieldId` update/remove field config
66	- `GET/PUT /groups/:id/my-fields` get and submit own field values
67	- US phone format validation on phone-type fields
68	- Notification emitted on member join/remove, field config change
69	- Integration tests: join, duplicate join 409, member removal, field config
70	
71	---
72	
73	## Slice 5 — Itinerary Items and Check-in
74	Done when:
75	- `GET/POST /groups/:groupId/itineraries` list and create items
76	- Meetup date validated as MM/DD/YYYY
77	- Meetup time validated as 12-hour format (HH:MM AM/PM)
78	- Notes max 2,000 chars enforced
79	- Idempotency key required on create/update
80	- `GET/PATCH/DELETE /groups/:groupId/itineraries/:itemId` get, update, delete
81	- `GET/POST /groups/:groupId/itineraries/:itemId/checkpoints` manage checkpoints (max 30)
82	- `POST /groups/:groupId/itineraries/:itemId/checkin` validates required fields → 400 with missing list if incomplete
83	- Notification emitted on item create/update/delete
84	- Transactional rollback on failed item create/update
85	- Unit tests: date/time format validation, checkpoint limit, required field check
86	- Integration tests: create, idempotency retry, checkin with missing fields 400, checkpoint max 400, unauthorized item access 403/404
87	
88	---
89	
90	## Slice 6 — File Management
91	Done when:
92	- `POST /groups/:groupId/files` uploads file (max 10 MB, MIME allowlist enforced)
93	- SHA-256 deduplication: returns existing file if hash matches
94	- `GET /groups/:groupId/files` lists files (members only)
95	- `GET /groups/:groupId/files/:fileId` downloads file (members only)
96	- `DELETE /groups/:groupId/files/:fileId` deletes file (owner/admin only → 403 otherwise)
97	- File access log on read and delete
98	- Notification emitted on file upload/delete
99	- Integration tests: upload, MIME rejection 400, size rejection 400, dedup, access control 403
100	
101	---
102	
103	## Slice 7 — Change Notifications
104	Done when:
105	- `GET /notifications?groupId=&after=&limit=` returns cursor-paginated notifications
106	- Notifications include: event_type, actor, resource_type, resource_id, detail, created_at
107	- Idempotency key on notification creation (duplicate = silent ignore)
108	- `PATCH /notifications/:id/read` marks notification as read
109	- Cursor is stable and monotonic under concurrent inserts
110	- Integration tests: cursor pagination, idempotency dedup, read marking, cross-group access blocked
111	
112	---
113	
114	## Slice 8 — Reporting and Analytics
115	Done when:
116	- `GET /reports/occupancy` — occupancy rate with day/week/month rollup
117	- `GET /reports/adr` — ADR with rollup
118	- `GET /reports/revpar` — RevPAR with rollup
119	- `GET /reports/revenue-mix` — revenue by channel and room type
120	- All reports support propertyId, from, to, groupBy filters
121	- Manager property-scope enforced (403 for other properties)
122	- `POST /reports/export` generates CSV or Excel locally, logs who/when/filters
123	- PII excluded from export unless explicit permission
124	- Unit tests: RevPAR formula, ADR formula, occupancy calculation
125	- Integration tests: report endpoints, export, cross-property 403, PII block
126	
127	---
128	
129	## Slice 9 — Data Import (Staffing and Evaluations)
130	Done when:
131	- `GET /import/templates/:datasetType` returns Excel template
132	- `POST /import/upload` parses Excel, validates columns, returns error receipt (row, field, reason)
133	- Duplicate merge: employee_id + effective_date → update existing record
134	- `POST /import/:batchId/commit` applies valid rows in a transaction (rollback on failure)
135	- `GET /import/:batchId` returns batch status and error receipt
136	- `GET /reports/staffing` — position distribution and staffing gaps
137	- `GET /reports/evaluations` — results, rewards/penalties, signed-off-by
138	- Retry failed imports: exponential backoff, max 3 attempts
139	- Unit tests: column validation, duplicate merge, rollback on error
140	- Integration tests: upload, commit, error receipt, staffing report, evaluation report
141	
142	---
143	
144	## Slice 10 — Face Enrollment
145	Done when:
146	- `POST /face/enroll/start` creates enrollment session
147	- `POST /face/enroll/:sessionId/capture` accepts angle + liveness metadata
148	- Liveness check: blink timing, motion consistency, texture/reflection heuristics
149	- `POST /face/enroll/:sessionId/complete` verifies all 3 angles + liveness, generates AES-256 encrypted template
150	- Raw image stored optionally, scheduled for hard-delete after 24h
151	- `GET /face/enrollments` lists own enrollments with version and status
152	- `PATCH /face/enrollments/:id` deactivates enrollment
153	- New enrollment creates new version, previous deactivated
154	- Unit tests: liveness scoring, template encryption, version increment
155	- Integration tests: full enrollment flow, incomplete angles 400, liveness fail 400
156	
157	---
158	
159	## Slice 11 — Data Quality and Observability
160	Done when:
161	- `GET/POST /quality/checks` manage quality check configs
162	- `POST /quality/checks/:id/run` runs check on demand with trace ID
163	- Scheduled hourly quality checks
164	- Null coverage, duplication ratio, outlier (configurable z-score) checks implemented
165	- `GET /quality/results` returns latest results
166	- Operational metrics stored in DB: job duration, queue depth, DB resource usage
167	- Integration tests: create config, run check, results endpoint
168	
169	---
170	
171	## Slice 12 — Audit Log and Security Hardening
172	Done when:
173	- AES-256-GCM encryption verified on face templates and sensitive fields
174	- Sensitive fields masked in all log output ([REDACTED])
175	- Append-only audit_logs (no DELETE for app DB role), 1-year retention
176	- `GET /audit-logs` query endpoint (Hotel Admin only)
177	- `GET /audit-logs/export` CSV export with masking
178	- Transactional boundaries verified on all import and itinerary operations
179	- Security-focused integration tests: auth entry points, role bypass attempts, object-level access denial, tenant isolation
180	- Tests/assertions confirm no password/token/secret leaks in API responses and logs
181	- Integration tests: audit log read, masking in export, 403 for non-admin
182	
183	---
184	
185	## Slice 13 — Final Polish
186	Done when:
187	- `run_tests.sh` passes all unit + integration tests
188	- `docker compose up` cold start works
189	- README has startup command, service addresses, test credentials
190	- No node_modules, dist, or compiled output in repo
191	- No real credentials in any config file
192	- Swagger UI available at `/api/docs`
193	- All p95 read queries have proper indexes
194	- Face image cleanup job verified
195	- Export archive cleanup job verified
196	- Static test coverage audit completed with Prompt Requirement Checklist mapping
197	- Acceptance report saved to `./.tmp/delivery-acceptance-report.md` with evidence `path:line`
198	
199	---
200	
201	## Acceptance Gate (After Slice 13)
202	Project is acceptance-ready only if:
203	- Mandatory thresholds pass: runnable verification or explicit environment-boundary statement.
204	- Core Prompt requirements are fully mapped to implementation artifacts.
205	- Security checks pass for authentication, route authorization, object authorization, and data isolation.
206	- Test Coverage Assessment (static audit) concludes at least `Partially Pass` with explicit risk boundaries.

2	# Hospitality Operations Intelligence — AI Self-Test Checklist
3	
4	This document is used to verify all prompt requirements are implemented before submission.
5	
6	Evidence rule for each checked item:
7	- Link implementation and verification evidence as `path:line`.
8	- Include reproducible command(s) and expected result.
9	- If blocked by environment constraints, mark as `Unconfirmed` with exact boundary.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Authentication and Accounts
14	
15	- [ ] `POST /auth/register` — creates user, enforces min 10 chars + 1 number + 1 symbol
16	- [ ] `POST /auth/login` — validates credentials, returns JWT
17	- [ ] `POST /auth/logout` — invalidates session
18	- [ ] `PATCH /auth/change-password` — enforces password policy

20	- [ ] `GET /accounts/me` — returns profile (legal name, US address, tax invoice title, preferred currency)
21	- [ ] `PATCH /accounts/me` — updates profile fields
22	- [ ] `POST /accounts/me/delete` — soft-deletes account after password confirmation
23	- [ ] `POST /accounts/me/export` — packages profile + activity logs + files into local ZIP archive
24	
25	---
26

34	- [ ] Member: itinerary-only access
35	- [ ] PII export blocked unless `pii_export_allowed` flag set
36	- [ ] Route-level authorization verified (protected route unauthenticated -> 401, wrong role -> 403)
37	- [ ] Object-level authorization verified (resource ownership/membership required, not only ID lookup)
38	- [ ] Cross-tenant data isolation verified (cross-group and cross-property reads/writes blocked)

67	- [ ] Max 30 checkpoints per item enforced (400 if exceeded)

115	- [ ] `POST /import/upload` — parses Excel, validates columns, returns error receipt (row, field, reason)

131	- [ ] Raw image stored optionally, hard-deleted after 24h by scheduled job
132	- [ ] `GET /face/enrollments` — lists own enrollments with version and status
133	- [ ] `PATCH /face/enrollments/:id` — deactivates enrollment
134	- [ ] New enrollment creates new version, previous deactivated

138	## Data Quality and Observability
139	
140	- [ ] `GET/POST /quality/checks` — manage quality check configs
141	- [ ] `POST /quality/checks/:id/run` — runs check on demand with trace ID
142	- [ ] Null coverage threshold check implemented
143	- [ ] Duplication ratio check implemented
144	- [ ] Outlier detection with configurable z-score bounds implemented
145	- [ ] Scheduled hourly quality checks

153	
154	- [ ] bcrypt rounds=12 on all passwords
155	- [ ] AES-256-GCM on face templates and sensitive fields
156	- [ ] Sensitive fields masked in all log output ([REDACTED])
157	- [ ] Append-only audit_logs (no DELETE for app DB role), 1-year retention
158	- [ ] `GET /audit-logs` — query endpoint (Hotel Admin only)
159	- [ ] `GET /audit-logs/export` — CSV export with masking

164	- [ ] Log channels/categories are consistent for auth/rbac/audit/import/reporting/system events

content.text:
1	# Strict Acceptance Checklist (Zero Partial Pass)
2	
3	Use this checklist repeatedly until every item is `Pass`.
4	Allowed final statuses: `Pass` or `Not Applicable` only.
5	Disallowed at release gate: `Partially Pass`, `Fail`, `Unconfirmed`.
6	
7	## How to Run This
8	
9	- Run gates in order.
10	- If any gate has non-pass items, stop, fix, and restart from Gate 1.
11	- Record evidence for every item as `path:line` and command output reference.
12	- Save each cycle report to `./.tmp/delivery-acceptance-report-cycle-<n>.md`.
13	
14	## Gate 1 — Security and Access Control (Stop-Ship)
15	
16	- [ ] Authentication entry points enforce expected behavior (`register`, `login`, `logout`, `change-password`).
17	- [ ] Route-level authorization is enforced (`401` unauthenticated, `403` unauthorized).
18	- [ ] Object-level authorization is enforced (ownership/membership checks on ID-based resources).
19	- [ ] Tenant/property/group isolation is enforced for read and write paths.
20	- [ ] Admin-only interfaces are protected and non-admin access is denied.
21	- [ ] No privilege escalation paths are found in role transitions or claims handling.
22	- [ ] Sensitive data is masked in logs and not exposed in API responses.
23	
24	Fail rule:
25	- Any single failure here = release blocked.
26	
27	## Gate 2 — Prompt Core Feature Completeness (Stop-Ship)
28	
29	- [ ] Auth/accounts features complete, including data export archive and account deletion.
30	- [ ] RBAC complete for all four roles and property-scoped rules.
31	- [ ] Group/join-code/member/required-field flows complete.
32	- [ ] Itinerary/checkpoint/check-in/idempotency/rollback flows complete.
33	- [ ] File upload/access/delete/dedup/audit flow complete.
34	- [ ] Notification creation, cursor pagination, and read-state flow complete.
35	- [ ] Reporting metrics and export constraints (PII permissions) complete.
36	- [ ] Import templates/validation/merge/error-receipts/retry/rollback complete.
37	- [ ] Face enrollment/liveness/encryption/version/deactivation/retention complete.
38	- [ ] Data quality checks, trace IDs, and metrics collection complete.
39	- [ ] Audit immutability/retention and masking requirements complete.
40	
41	Fail rule:
42	- Any missing core prompt feature = release blocked.
43	
44	## Gate 3 — Engineering Reliability (Stop-Ship)
45	
46	- [ ] Project starts with documented commands and no source edits required.
47	- [ ] Error handling is consistent and actionable across modules.
48	- [ ] Input validation exists on all external boundaries.
49	- [ ] Transaction boundaries protect imports and itinerary edits.
50	- [ ] Retry logic uses bounded exponential backoff (max 3 attempts).
51	- [ ] Logging categories are clear and traceable (`auth`, `rbac`, `audit`, `import`, `reporting`, `security`, `system`).
52	- [ ] Offline-only operation has no external runtime dependency.
53	
54	## Gate 4 — Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit, Mandatory)
55	
56	- [ ] Requirement checklist extracted from Prompt and constraints.
57	- [ ] Mapping table complete: requirement -> test case -> assertion -> judgment.
58	- [ ] Happy paths covered for all critical user/business journeys.
59	- [ ] Exception paths covered (`400/401/403/404/409`, validation, duplicates, not-found).
60	- [ ] Security paths covered (authn, route authz, object authz, data isolation).
61	- [ ] Boundary paths covered (time/date, pagination, empty sets, extremes, concurrency/idempotency).
62	- [ ] Transaction/rollback and retry behavior covered.
63	- [ ] Log/sensitive info leakage risk audited in code/tests.
64	- [ ] Mock/stub scope and production activation risk documented.
65	
66	Pass rule:
67	- Overall test-audit conclusion must be `Pass` (not `Partially Pass`).
68	
69	## Gate 5 — Final Readiness
70	
71	- [ ] README includes exact startup, test, and verification commands.
72	- [ ] API spec and docs match implemented behavior.
73	- [ ] No forbidden artifacts for delivery (`node_modules`, `dist`, real credentials, temp exports/uploads).
74	- [ ] Evidence-backed acceptance report generated at `./.tmp/delivery-acceptance-report.md`.
75	- [ ] All issues closed or explicitly `Not Applicable` with boundary rationale.
76	
77	## Retest Loop (Repeat Until Pass)
78	
79	For each failed item:
80	1. Create a minimal fix scoped to the failed requirement.
81	2. Re-run targeted checks for that requirement.
82	3. Re-run all prior gates (security first).
83	4. Update cycle report and evidence links.
84	
85	Exit condition:
86	- Two consecutive full acceptance cycles with all items `Pass`.
87

content.text:
1	
2	# Questions and Clarifications — Hospitality Operations Intelligence
3	
4	---
5	
6	## 1. Password Policy — What Counts as a Symbol?
7	
8	**Question:** The prompt says passwords must have at least 1 number and 1 symbol. What characters qualify as a symbol?
9	
10	**Assumption:** Any non-alphanumeric printable ASCII character qualifies: `!@#$%^&*()_+-=[]{}|;':",.<>?/~\``. Standard special character set.
11	
12	**Solution:** Zod/regex validation: `/^(?=.*\d)(?=.*[^a-zA-Z0-9]).{10,}$/`. Applied at registration and password change.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## 2. Account Lockout — How Many Attempts and How Long?
17	
18	**Question:** The prompt mentions account lockout but doesn't specify the threshold or duration.
19	
20	**Assumption:** Lock after 5 consecutive failed login attempts. Lockout duration: 15 minutes. After lockout expires, failed_attempts resets on next successful login.
21	
22	**Solution:** `users.failed_attempts` incremented on each failure. `users.locked_until` set to `now + 15 min` when threshold reached. Login checks `locked_until > now` → 423 response.
23	
24	---
25	
26	## 3. Join Code — How Is It Generated and How Long Is It Valid?
27	
28	**Question:** The prompt says groups use a "pre-shared join code" but doesn't specify format, length, or expiry.
29	
30	**Assumption:** Join codes are 8-character alphanumeric strings (uppercase), generated at group creation. They do not expire — they remain valid as long as the group is active. Archiving a group invalidates its join code.
31	
32	**Solution:** `groups.join_code` generated with `crypto.randomBytes(4).toString('hex').toUpperCase()`. Unique constraint enforced. Archived groups return 404 on join attempt.
33	
34	---
35	
36	## 4. Itinerary Date/Time Format — Server-Side or Display Only?
37	
38	**Question:** The prompt specifies MM/DD/YYYY and 12-hour time formats. Are these stored as-is or converted to ISO format internally?
39	
40	**Assumption:** Store the client-facing values exactly as provided (`MM/DD/YYYY`, `HH:MM AM/PM`) for strict format fidelity, and also store a derived normalized datetime field for ordering/filtering safety.
41	
42	**Solution:** Keep `meetup_date` (`varchar(10)`) + `meetup_time` (`varchar(8)`) with regex validation, and add `meetup_sort_at` (`datetime`) derived server-side from those values. API responses preserve the original prompt-required format.
43	
44	---
45	
46	## 5. Checkpoint Ordering — Can Positions Be Reordered?
47	
48	**Question:** Checkpoints have an ordered position (1–30). Can positions be reordered after creation, or is position immutable?
49	
50	**Assumption:** Positions can be updated via PATCH on a checkpoint. The service validates that no two checkpoints on the same item share the same position. Reordering is done by updating position values.
51	
52	**Solution:** `PATCH /groups/:groupId/itineraries/:itemId/checkpoints/:checkpointId` accepts `{position}`. Service checks for position conflicts before saving.
53	
54	---
55	
56	## 6. File MIME Allowlist — Exact Types?
57	
58	**Question:** The prompt says "image/document upload" with MIME-type allowlists. What exact MIME types are allowed?
59	
60	**Assumption:** Images: `image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/gif`, `image/webp`. Documents: `application/pdf`, `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` (docx), `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet` (xlsx).
61	
62	**Solution:** Allowlist defined in config. Multer middleware checks `file.mimetype` against allowlist before accepting upload. Returns 400 with code `MIME_NOT_ALLOWED` if rejected.
63	
64	---
65	
66	## 7. SHA-256 Deduplication — Scope: Per User or Global?
67	
68	**Question:** When a file's SHA-256 matches an existing file, is deduplication global (any user) or per-group?
69	
70	**Assumption:** Deduplication is global for storage efficiency, but API behavior must avoid cross-group information leakage.
71	
72	**Solution:** `files.sha256` has a unique index. On hash match, create/ensure group association and return a generic success payload that does not disclose whether bytes already existed in another group. Access remains enforced by group membership checks.
73	
74	---
75	
76	## 8. Reporting — What Is the Room Availability Denominator for RevPAR?
77	
78	**Question:** RevPAR = total revenue / available room nights. How is "available" defined — all rooms, or only non-maintenance rooms?
79	
80	**Assumption:** Available room nights = all rooms with status `available` or `occupied` (i.e., excluding `maintenance`). A room under maintenance is not available for sale and should not inflate the denominator.
81	
82	**Solution:** RevPAR denominator counts rooms where `status != 'maintenance'` for each day in the period, multiplied by the number of days.
83	
84	---
85	
86	## 9. PII Export Permission — How Is It Granted?
87	
88	**Question:** The prompt says PII export requires "explicit permission grant." How is this permission managed?
89	
90	**Assumption:** PII export is a separate permission flag on the user record (`pii_export_allowed: boolean`). Only Hotel Admin can grant this flag to other users. Analyst role can run reports but cannot export PII unless the flag is set.
91	
92	**Solution:** `users.pii_export_allowed` boolean field. Report export endpoint checks this flag when `includePii: true` is requested. Returns 403 if flag is false.
93	
94	---
95	
96	## 10. Face Liveness — What Are the Pass Thresholds?
97	
98	**Question:** The prompt describes liveness checks using blink timing, motion consistency, and texture/reflection heuristics. What are the pass/fail thresholds?
99	
100	**Assumption:** Configurable thresholds with sensible defaults: blink timing 100–500ms (natural blink range), motion consistency score ≥ 0.6, texture/reflection score ≥ 0.5. All three must pass for a capture to be accepted.
101	
102	**Solution:** Thresholds defined in environment config. Liveness service evaluates each metric and returns a per-metric pass/fail plus an overall result. Any single failure rejects the capture.
103	
104	---
105	
106	## 11. Face Template — What Format Is the Encrypted Template?
107	
108	**Question:** The prompt says "only encrypted face templates are stored." What is the template format before encryption?
109	
110	**Assumption:** For this implementation, the face template is a JSON object containing the liveness metadata and capture scores for all three angles. It is serialized to JSON, then encrypted with AES-256-GCM. The raw image is not part of the template.
111	
112	**Solution:** Template = `JSON.stringify({angles: {left: {...}, front: {...}, right: {...}}, enrolledAt: ...})`. Encrypted with AES-256-GCM using key from env. Stored as base64 string in `face_enrollments.template_path` (or as a file in `face-templates/`).
113	
114	---
115	
116	## 12. Notification Cursor — What Is the Cursor Format?
117	
118	**Question:** The prompt says notifications are queryable by cursor. What is the cursor — a timestamp, an ID, or an offset?
119	
120	**Assumption:** Cursor should be monotonic and stable under concurrent inserts; UUID lexical comparison is not reliable.
121	
122	**Solution:** Use opaque cursor encoding `{created_at,id}` (base64 JSON). Query with `(created_at > ts) OR (created_at = ts AND id > id)` ordered by `created_at ASC, id ASC`. Endpoint stays `GET /notifications?groupId=&after=<cursor>&limit=50`, returning `nextCursor` with the same format.
123	
124	---
125	
126	## 13. Data Export Archive — What Format?
127	
128	**Question:** The prompt says account data export packages profile, activity logs, and uploaded files into a "locally generated archive." What format?
129	
130	**Assumption:** ZIP archive containing: `profile.json` (user profile), `activity_logs.json` (all activity log entries), `files/` directory with copies of uploaded files. Archive is stored in `exports/` directory and served via a time-limited local URL.
131	
132	**Solution:** Node.js `archiver` library creates the ZIP. Archive expires and is deleted after 24 hours by the cleanup job. Download URL is a local API endpoint: `GET /exports/:archiveId`.
133	
134	---
135	
136	## 14. Staffing Import — What Columns Are Required?
137	
138	**Question:** The prompt says strict column validation for staffing and evaluation imports. What are the required columns?
139	
140	**Assumption:**
141	- Staffing: `employee_id` (required), `effective_date` (required, YYYY-MM-DD), `position` (required), `department` (optional), `property_id` (optional), `signed_off_by` (optional)
142	- Evaluation: `employee_id` (required), `effective_date` (required), `score` (required, decimal), `result` (required), `rewards` (optional), `penalties` (optional), `signed_off_by` (optional)
143	
144	**Solution:** Column schema defined per dataset type. Import service validates presence and type of each column. Missing required columns → batch-level error (not row-level). Wrong type → row-level error.
145	
146	---
147	
148	## 15. Audit Log Retention — 1 Year — Hard Delete or Archive?
149	
150	**Question:** The prompt says audit logs are immutable for 1 year. Does this mean rows are never deleted, or can they be archived after 1 year?
151	
152	**Assumption:** Audit log rows are never deleted from the primary `audit_logs` table within the 1-year retention window. The app DB role has INSERT-only permission on `audit_logs`. After 1 year, rows may be archived to a cold table but are not hard-deleted from the system.
153	
154	**Solution:** MySQL role for the app has INSERT-only on `audit_logs`. A daily job checks for rows older than 1 year and moves them to `audit_logs_archive`. The primary table retains all rows within the 1-year window.
155	
156	---
157	
158	## 16. Manager Property Scope — Can a Manager Be Assigned to Multiple Properties?
159	
160	**Question:** The prompt says managers have "property-scoped access." Can a manager be assigned to more than one property?
161	
162	**Assumption:** A manager is assigned to exactly one property (stored as `users.property_id`). If multi-property manager access is needed, it would require a separate `user_properties` join table, but the prompt implies single-property scope.
163	
164	**Solution:** `users.property_id` is a single FK. All manager queries filter by this property_id. Hotel Admin has no property filter.
165	
166	---
167	
168	## 17. Idempotency on Itinerary Updates — What Is the Scope?
169	
170	**Question:** The prompt says idempotency keys prevent duplicate updates during retries. Is the key scoped per item, per user, or globally?
171	
172	**Assumption:** Idempotency keys are scoped by actor + operation + target resource and retained for 24 hours.
173	
174	**Solution:** Use a dedicated `idempotency_keys` table (`key`, `actor_id`, `operation`, `resource_scope`, `request_hash`, `response_snapshot`, `expires_at`). On duplicate key with same request hash, return stored response; mismatched hash returns 409. Avoid storing update idempotency directly on `itinerary_items` rows.
175

1	# Delivery Acceptance / Project Architecture Review Plan
2	
3	This plan aligns project acceptance with the stated scoring criteria and hard rules.
4	
5	## Execution Principles
6	
7	- Evaluate item-by-item against acceptance criteria only.
8	- Prioritize security verification (authn/authz/object authorization/data isolation) above style concerns.
9	- Provide traceable evidence for every key conclusion (`path:line`).
10	- Prefer runnable verification; if blocked by environment limits, document command + verification boundary.
11	- Record final report in `./.tmp/delivery-acceptance-report.md`.
12	- Target quality bar is strict acceptance: no `Partially Pass` at release.
13	- Repeat acceptance cycles until results are stable across consecutive runs.
14	
15	## Major Acceptance Checklist (Plan)
16	
17	- [ ] 1. Mandatory Thresholds (runnability + Prompt-theme alignment)
18	- [ ] 2. Delivery Completeness (all core Prompt requirements + real deliverable form)
19	- [ ] 3. Engineering and Architecture Quality (structure, modularity, maintainability)
20	- [ ] 4. Engineering Details and Professionalism (validation, error handling, logs, interfaces)
21	- [ ] 5. Prompt Understanding and Fitness (business goal fidelity, constraints respected)
22	- [ ] 6. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit) + risk boundary conclusion
23	- [ ] 7. Final zero-partial-pass gate (all critical items fully pass)
24	
25	Strict mode reference checklist:
26	- `docs/acceptance-checklist-strict.md`
27	
28	## Required Verification Content Per Major Item
29	
30	For each sub-item:
31	- Conclusion: `Pass` / `Partially Pass` / `Fail` / `Not Applicable` / `Unconfirmed`
32	- Reason: benchmark clause + engineering basis
33	- Evidence: implementation and docs `path:line`
34	- Verification: command/steps + expected result
35	
36	For any `Not Applicable`:
37	- State why it does not apply and the judgment boundary.
38	
39	## Security Priority Verification (Mandatory)
40	
41	Must explicitly verify:
42	- Authentication entry points (registration/login/token handling/logout/password change)
43	- Route-level authorization (role checks and protected endpoints)
44	- Object-level authorization (resource ownership/membership checks)
45	- Data isolation (group/property/tenant boundaries)
46	- Admin/debug interface protections
47	
48	Report each issue with:
49	- Priority (`Blocking` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`)
50	- Impact
51	- Evidence (`path:line`)
52	- Minimal reproducible path
53	- Minimal actionable fix suggestion
54	
55	## Static Test Coverage Assessment (Mandatory Section)
56	
57	The report must include a separate section named `Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)` with:
58	
59	- Test overview: unit/API/integration test existence, framework, entry command, README executability notes.
60	- Requirement mapping table:
61	  - `Requirement/Risk Point`
62	  - `Corresponding Test Case (file:line)`
63	  - `Key Assertion/Fixture/Mock (file:line)`
64	  - `Coverage Judgment`
65	  - `Gap`
66	  - `Minimal Test Addition Suggestion`
67	- Coverage judgment for:
68	  - Happy paths
69	  - Exception paths (400/401/403/404/409/idempotency)
70	  - Security paths (authn/authz/object authz/data isolation)
71	  - Boundaries (pagination/filtering/empty/extreme/time/concurrency/transactions)
72	  - Logs and sensitive info leakage risk
73	- Mock/stub notes:
74	  - Scope, activation conditions, accidental-production risk
75	- Overall conclusion (must be one): `Pass` / `Partially Pass` / `Fail` / `Unconfirmed`
76	- Explicit judgment boundary: what severe defects could still pass current tests
77	
78	## Reproducible Command Set (No Docker Execution Here)
79	
80	Use these commands during acceptance as applicable (without starting Docker in this environment):
81	
82	```bash
83	# Documentation and project structure check
84	ls
85	ls docs
86	
87	# Node/TypeScript project checks
88	npm run lint
89	npm run build
90	npm test
91	
92	# Optional targeted tests
93	npm run test:unit
94	npm run test:integration
95	```
96	
97	If command execution is blocked, report:
98	- exact error
99	- likely environmental cause
100	- what remains statically confirmable from code/docs
101	
102	## Final Output Requirements
103	
104	- Include prioritized issue list (`Blocking` to `Low`) with impact and evidence.
105	- Separate conclusions for:
106	  - Unit test audit
107	  - API/integration test audit
108	  - Log classification/sensitive info audit
109	- Do not report sandbox permission limits as project defects.
110	- Do not report compliant payment mocks as defects.
111	
112	## Release Gate (Strict)
113	
114	Release can proceed only if all conditions hold:
115	- Every mandatory item is `Pass`.
116	- `Partially Pass` count is `0`.
117	- `Fail` count is `0`.
118	- `Unconfirmed` count is `0` (except explicitly environment-blocked items with reproducible command + boundary, then release is deferred).
119	- Security section has zero open issues in `Blocking` and `High`.
120	- Static test coverage conclusion is `Pass`.
121	
122	Recommended stabilization rule:
123	- Require two consecutive full acceptance runs with identical `Pass` outcomes before final sign-off.
124

1	# Hospitality Operations Intelligence — Design Document
2	
3	## 1. Overview
4	
5	Hospitality Operations Intelligence is an offline-first backend API platform for hotel groups and property management teams. It handles guest itinerary coordination, operational reporting, staffing data imports, and face enrollment. No UI. Pure REST API built with Express (TypeScript) + Sequelize + MySQL. Runs on a single Docker host with no external connectivity.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 2. Architecture
10	
11	```
12	HTTP Client (Postman / frontend)
13	  │
14	  ▼
15	Express HTTP Server (port 3000)
16	  ├── Global Error Handler          → structured JSON errors
17	  ├── Trace ID Middleware           → X-Trace-Id on every request
18	  ├── JWT Auth Middleware           → validates Bearer token
19	  ├── RBAC Middleware               → role + property-scope enforcement
20	  ├── Audit Middleware              → append-only audit trail
21	  ├── Rate Limit Middleware         → per-IP and per-user limits
22	  └── Domain Controllers/Services
23	        │
24	        ▼
25	   Sequelize ORM layer
26	        │
27	        ▼
28	   MySQL 8 (port 3306)
29	        │
30	   Local filesystem (uploads, exports, face-templates)
31	```
32	
33	---
34	
35	## 3. Technology Stack
36	
37	| Layer | Choice |
38	|---|---|
39	| HTTP framework | Express (TypeScript) |
40	| ORM | Sequelize |
41	| Database | MySQL 8 |
42	| Auth | JWT (local, no external IdP) |
43	| Password hashing | bcrypt (rounds=12) |
44	| Field encryption | AES-256-GCM |
45	| Validation | zod |
46	| Scheduling | node-cron |
47	| API docs | Swagger UI (swagger-ui-express) |
48	| Logging | Winston with structured JSON |
49	| Excel/CSV | exceljs + csv-parse |
50	| File upload | multer |
51	| Container | Docker + docker-compose |
52	
53	---
54	
55	## 4. Module Responsibilities
56	
57	| Module | Responsibility |
58	|---|---|
59	| `auth` | Login, JWT, password policy, account lockout |
60	| `accounts` | Profile management, self-service deletion, data export |
61	| `groups` | Itinerary groups, join codes, member management |
62	| `itineraries` | Itinerary items, checkpoints, required fields, check-in |
63	| `files` | Upload, MIME validation, SHA-256 dedup, access control |
64	| `notifications` | In-system event records, cursor-based query, idempotency |
65	| `reports` | Occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, revenue mix, rollups, CSV/Excel export |
66	| `import` | Excel staffing/evaluation import, column validation, error receipts |
67	| `face` | Guided capture metadata, liveness checks, encrypted template storage |
68	| `quality` | Integrity checks, null coverage, duplication ratios, outlier detection |
69	| `audit` | Append-only audit log, 1-year immutability, sensitive field masking |
70	| `common` | Middleware, encryption, error handling, trace IDs |
71	
72	---
73	
74	## 5. Data Model
75	
76	### Auth and Accounts
77	
78	```
79	users
80	  id              varchar(36) PK
81	  username        varchar(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL
82	  password_hash   varchar(255) NOT NULL          -- bcrypt rounds=12
83	  role            enum NOT NULL                  -- hotel_admin | manager | analyst | member
84	  property_id     varchar(36) FK properties (nullable) -- for manager scope
85	  legal_name      varchar(255)
86	  address_line1   varchar(255)
87	  address_line2   varchar(255)
88	  city            varchar(100)
89	  state           varchar(2)                     -- US state code
90	  zip             varchar(10)
91	  tax_invoice_title varchar(255)
92	  preferred_currency varchar(3)                  -- ISO 4217
93	  status          enum DEFAULT active            -- active | suspended | deleted
94	  failed_attempts int DEFAULT 0
95	  locked_until    datetime
96	  created_at      datetime
97	  updated_at      datetime
98	  deleted_at      datetime                       -- soft delete
99	
100	activity_logs
101	  id              varchar(36) PK
102	  user_id         varchar(36) FK users
103	  action          varchar(255) NOT NULL
104	  detail          json
105	  trace_id        varchar(36)
106	  created_at      datetime NOT NULL
107	```
108	
109	### Properties
110	
111	```
112	properties
113	  id              varchar(36) PK
114	  name            varchar(255) NOT NULL
115	  address         text
116	  created_at      datetime
117	```
118	
119	### Itinerary Groups
120	
121	```
122	groups
123	  id              varchar(36) PK
124	  name            varchar(255) NOT NULL
125	  owner_id        varchar(36) FK users
126	  join_code       varchar(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL    -- pre-shared join code
127	  status          enum DEFAULT active            -- active | archived
128	  created_at      datetime
129	  updated_at      datetime
130	
131	group_members
132	  id              varchar(36) PK
133	  group_id        varchar(36) FK groups
134	  user_id         varchar(36) FK users
135	  role            enum DEFAULT member            -- owner | admin | member
136	  joined_at       datetime
137	  UNIQUE (group_id, user_id)
138	
139	group_required_fields
140	  id              varchar(36) PK
141	  group_id        varchar(36) FK groups
142	  field_name      varchar(100) NOT NULL          -- e.g. vehicle_make, emergency_contact_name
143	  field_type      varchar(50) NOT NULL           -- text | phone | select
144	  is_required     boolean DEFAULT true
145	  created_at      datetime
146	
147	member_field_values
148	  id              varchar(36) PK
149	  group_id        varchar(36) FK groups
150	  user_id         varchar(36) FK users
151	  field_name      varchar(100) NOT NULL
152	  value           text NOT NULL
153	  created_at      datetime
154	  updated_at      datetime
155	  UNIQUE (group_id, user_id, field_name)
156	```
157	
158	### Itinerary Items
159	
160	```
161	itinerary_items
162	  id              varchar(36) PK
163	  group_id        varchar(36) FK groups
164	  title           varchar(255) NOT NULL
165	  meetup_date     varchar(10) NOT NULL           -- MM/DD/YYYY
166	  meetup_time     varchar(8) NOT NULL            -- HH:MM AM/PM (12-hour)
167	  meetup_location text NOT NULL
168	  notes           text                           -- max 2,000 chars
169	  created_by      varchar(36) FK users
170	  idempotency_key varchar(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL
171	  created_at      datetime
172	  updated_at      datetime
173	
174	itinerary_checkpoints
175	  id              varchar(36) PK
176	  item_id         varchar(36) FK itinerary_items
177	  position        int NOT NULL                   -- 1-30, ordered
178	  label           varchar(255) NOT NULL
179	  description     text
180	  created_at      datetime
181	
182	member_checkins
183	  id              varchar(36) PK
184	  item_id         varchar(36) FK itinerary_items
185	  user_id         varchar(36) FK users
186	  checked_in_at   datetime NOT NULL
187	  UNIQUE (item_id, user_id)
188	```
189	
190	### Files
191	
192	```
193	files
194	  id              varchar(36) PK
195	  group_id        varchar(36) FK groups (nullable)
196	  uploaded_by     varchar(36) FK users
197	  original_name   varchar(255) NOT NULL
198	  mime_type       varchar(100) NOT NULL
199	  size_bytes      int NOT NULL
200	  sha256          varchar(64) NOT NULL           -- for deduplication
201	  storage_path    varchar(500) NOT NULL
202	  created_at      datetime
203	
204	file_access_log
205	  id              varchar(36) PK
206	  file_id         varchar(36) FK files
207	  user_id         varchar(36) FK users
208	  action          varchar(50) NOT NULL           -- read | delete
209	  created_at      datetime
210	```
211	
212	### Notifications
213	
214	```
215	notifications
216	  id              varchar(36) PK
217	  group_id        varchar(36) FK groups
218	  actor_id        varchar(36) FK users
219	  event_type      varchar(100) NOT NULL          -- item_created | item_updated | member_joined | etc.
220	  resource_type   varchar(100)
221	  resource_id     varchar(36)
222	  detail          json                           -- what changed
223	  idempotency_key varchar(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL
224	  created_at      datetime NOT NULL
225	
226	notification_reads
227	  notification_id varchar(36) FK notifications
228	  user_id         varchar(36) FK users
229	  read_at         datetime
230	  PRIMARY KEY (notification_id, user_id)
231	```
232	
233	### Hospitality / Reporting
234	
235	```
236	properties
237	  (see above)
238	
239	rooms
240	  id              varchar(36) PK
241	  property_id     varchar(36) FK properties
242	  room_number     varchar(20) NOT NULL
243	  room_type       varchar(100) NOT NULL
244	  rate_cents      int NOT NULL
245	  status          enum DEFAULT available         -- available | occupied | maintenance
246	  created_at      datetime
247	
248	reservations
249	  id              varchar(36) PK
250	  property_id     varchar(36) FK properties
251	  room_id         varchar(36) FK rooms
252	  guest_name      varchar(255) NOT NULL
253	  channel         varchar(100) NOT NULL          -- direct | ota | corporate | group
254	  check_in_date   date NOT NULL
255	  check_out_date  date NOT NULL
256	  rate_cents      int NOT NULL
257	  status          enum DEFAULT confirmed         -- confirmed | checked_in | checked_out | cancelled
258	  created_at      datetime
259	  updated_at      datetime
260	```
261	
262	### Staffing Import
263	
264	```
265	import_batches
266	  id              varchar(36) PK
267	  user_id         varchar(36) FK users
268	  batch_type      varchar(50) NOT NULL           -- staffing | evaluation
269	  status          enum DEFAULT pending           -- pending | processing | completed | failed
270	  total_rows      int DEFAULT 0
271	  success_rows    int DEFAULT 0
272	  error_rows      int DEFAULT 0
273	  trace_id        varchar(36)
274	  created_at      datetime
275	  completed_at    datetime
276	
277	import_errors
278	  id              varchar(36) PK
279	  batch_id        varchar(36) FK import_batches
280	  row_number      int NOT NULL
281	  field           varchar(255)
282	  reason          text NOT NULL
283	  raw_data        json
284	
285	staffing_records
286	  id              varchar(36) PK
287	  batch_id        varchar(36) FK import_batches
288	  employee_id     varchar(100) NOT NULL
289	  effective_date  date NOT NULL
290	  position        varchar(255) NOT NULL
291	  department      varchar(255)
292	  property_id     varchar(36) FK properties
293	  signed_off_by   varchar(255)
294	  created_at      datetime
295	  UNIQUE (employee_id, effective_date)           -- dedup merge key
296	
297	evaluation_records
298	  id              varchar(36) PK
299	  batch_id        varchar(36) FK import_batches
300	  employee_id     varchar(100) NOT NULL
301	  effective_date  date NOT NULL
302	  score           decimal(5,2)
303	  result          varchar(100)
304	  rewards         text
305	  penalties       text
306	  signed_off_by   varchar(255)
307	  created_at      datetime
308	  UNIQUE (employee_id, effective_date)
309	```
310	
311	### Face Enrollment
312	
313	```
314	face_enrollments
315	  id              varchar(36) PK
316	  user_id         varchar(36) FK users
317	  version         int NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
318	  status          enum DEFAULT active            -- active | deactivated
319	  template_path   varchar(500) NOT NULL          -- path to AES-256 encrypted template
320	  angles_captured json NOT NULL                  -- {left: bool, front: bool, right: bool}
321	  liveness_passed boolean NOT NULL
322	  liveness_meta   json                           -- blink timing, motion, texture scores
323	  raw_image_path  varchar(500)                   -- nullable, deleted after 24h
324	  raw_image_expires_at datetime
325	  created_at      datetime
326	  updated_at      datetime
327	```
328	
329	### Data Quality
330	
331	```
332	quality_checks
333	  id              varchar(36) PK
334	  entity_type     varchar(100) NOT NULL
335	  check_type      varchar(100) NOT NULL          -- null_coverage | duplication_ratio | outlier
336	  config          json NOT NULL                  -- thresholds, z-score bounds
337	  result          json                           -- last run result
338	  passed          boolean
339	  run_at          datetime
340	  trace_id        varchar(36)
341	```
342	
343	### Audit
344	
345	```
346	audit_logs
347	  id              varchar(36) PK
348	  actor_id        varchar(36) FK users
349	  action          varchar(255) NOT NULL
350	  resource_type   varchar(100)
351	  resource_id     varchar(36)
352	  detail          json                           -- sensitive fields masked
353	  trace_id        varchar(36)
354	  ip_address      varchar(45)
355	  created_at      datetime NOT NULL
356	  -- INSERT only, no UPDATE/DELETE for app DB role
357	  -- retained for 1 year minimum
358	```
359	
360	---
361	
362	## 6. Key Flows
363	
364	### Group Join via Code
365	
366	```
367	1. POST /groups/join {joinCode}
368	2. Look up group by join_code
369	3. Check group.status = active
370	4. Check user not already a member
371	5. INSERT group_members (role=member)
372	6. Emit notification event: member_joined
373	7. Return 200 + group details
374	```
375	
376	### Itinerary Check-in
377	
378	```
379	1. POST /itineraries/:itemId/checkin
380	2. Load group required fields for item's group
381	3. Load member_field_values for current user
382	4. Check all is_required fields have values → else 400 with missing fields list
383	5. INSERT member_checkins
384	6. Return 200
385	```
386	
387	### Reporting — RevPAR Calculation
388	
389	```
390	RevPAR = Total Room Revenue / Total Available Room Nights
391	
392	1. GET /reports/revpar?propertyId=&from=&to=&groupBy=day|week|month
393	2. Filter reservations by property + date range + status=checked_out|checked_in
394	3. Sum rate_cents per period
395	4. Count available room nights per period (rooms × days)
396	5. Compute RevPAR per period
397	6. Return time-series array with rollup
398	```
399	
400	### Face Enrollment Flow
401	
402	```
403	1. POST /face/enroll/start — create enrollment session
404	2. POST /face/enroll/:sessionId/capture {angle: left|front|right, metadata: {...}}
405	   - Validate liveness: blink timing, motion consistency, texture/reflection heuristics
406	   - Store capture metadata
407	3. POST /face/enroll/:sessionId/complete
408	   - Verify all 3 angles captured
409	   - Verify liveness passed for all angles
410	   - Generate encrypted face template (AES-256)
411	   - Store template, schedule raw image deletion (24h)
412	   - INSERT face_enrollments
413	4. Return enrollment ID
414	```
415	
416	### Notification Cursor Query
417	
418	```
419	1. GET /notifications?groupId=&after=<cursor>&limit=50
420	2. cursor = last notification ID seen by client
421	3. SELECT * FROM notifications WHERE group_id=? AND id > cursor ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 50
422	4. Return notifications + next cursor
423	5. Idempotency: duplicate event with same idempotency_key is silently ignored
424	```
425	
426	### Data Export (Account)
427	
428	```
429	1. POST /accounts/export
430	2. Collect: user profile, activity_logs, uploaded files list
431	3. Package into ZIP archive (locally generated)
432	4. Store archive in exports/ directory
433	5. Return download URL (local path)
434	6. Log export event in audit_log
435	```
436	
437	---
438	
439	## 7. Security Design
440	
441	- Passwords: bcrypt rounds=12, min 10 chars, at least 1 number + 1 symbol
442	- JWT: HS256, secret from env, configurable TTL (default 8h)
443	- Field encryption: AES-256-GCM for face templates, sensitive profile fields
444	- Audit log: INSERT-only for app DB role, 1-year retention
445	- Property-scoped access: Manager queries always filtered by property_id
446	- PII export: requires explicit `pii_export` permission grant
447	- Sensitive fields masked in all log output (password_hash, face template paths)
448	- Trace IDs: UUID per request, attached to all log lines and `X-Trace-Id` header
449	- File access: group members only for read; owner/admin only for delete
450	- Raw face images: hard-deleted after 24 hours via scheduled job
451	
452	---
453	
454	## 8. Background Jobs
455	
456	| Job | Interval | Description |
457	|---|---|---|
458	| Face image cleanup | 15 min | Hard-delete raw face images past 24h retention |
459	| Import retry | 5 min | Retry failed import batches (max 3, exponential backoff) |
460	| Audit log retention check | daily | Alert if audit logs older than 1 year exist without archive |
461	| Quality checks | 1 hour | Run configured integrity checks per entity type |
462	| Export cleanup | 1 hour | Delete generated export archives older than 24h |
463	
464	---
465	
466	## 9. Error Handling
467	
468	All errors return:
469	```json
470	{
471	  "statusCode": 400,
472	  "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
473	  "message": "human readable message",
474	  "traceId": "uuid"
475	}
476	```
477	
478	Standard codes: VALIDATION_ERROR (400), UNAUTHORIZED (401), FORBIDDEN (403), NOT_FOUND (404), CONFLICT (409), IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT (409), INTERNAL_ERROR (500)
479	
480	---
481	
482	## 10. Docker Setup
483	
484	```yaml
485	services:
486	  api:
487	    build: .
488	    ports:
489	      - "3000:3000"
490	    environment:
491	      DATABASE_URL: mysql://hospitality:hospitality@db:3306/hospitality
492	      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET}
493	      ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${ENCRYPTION_KEY}
494	    volumes:
495	      - ./uploads:/app/uploads
496	      - ./exports:/app/exports
497	      - ./face-templates:/app/face-templates
498	    depends_on:
499	      db:
500	        condition: service_healthy
501	
502	  db:
503	    image: mysql:8
504	    environment:
505	      MYSQL_USER: hospitality
506	      MYSQL_PASSWORD: hospitality
507	      MYSQL_DATABASE: hospitality
508	      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: rootpassword
509	    healthcheck:
510	      test: ["CMD", "mysqladmin", "ping", "-h", "localhost"]
511	      interval: 5s
512	      retries: 10
513	    volumes:
514	      - mysqldata:/var/lib/mysql
515	
516	volumes:
517	  mysqldata:
518	```
519	
520	---
521	
522	## 11. Performance Strategy
523	
524	- Index all foreign keys
525	- Index `reservations.check_in_date`, `reservations.check_out_date`, `reservations.property_id`
526	- Index `audit_logs.created_at`, `audit_logs.actor_id`
527	- Index `notifications.group_id`, `notifications.created_at` (for cursor pagination)
528	- Index `staffing_records.employee_id`, `staffing_records.effective_date`
529	- Index `files.sha256` (for deduplication lookup)
530	- Sequelize query builder for complex reporting aggregations
531	- Connection pool: min 2, max 10
532	- Import batches processed in chunks of 500 rows
533	- Reporting queries use DB-level aggregation (SUM, COUNT, GROUP BY) not in-memory
534

I've read all the docs. Here's my analysis of gaps and inconsistencies. I'll fix them now.

---

**Key issues found:**

1. **`design.md` data model** — Missing tables: `face_enrollment_sessions`, `operational_metrics`, `export_logs`. Missing columns: `pii_export_allowed` on users, `meetup_sort_at` on itinerary_items. Missing `property.model.ts` in structure.
2. **`questions.md` Q17** — Proposes a separate `idempotency_keys` table, but design.md has `idempotency_key` inline on `itinerary_items`. Contradictory.
3. **`questions.md` Q7** — SHA-256 unique globally but `group_id` is on `files` table directly — can't share one file across groups without a join table.
4. **`questions.md` Q12 vs design.md** — Notification cursor: design says `id > cursor`, questions.md says composite `{created_at, id}`. Need to reconcile.
5. **`api-spec.md`** — Missing endpoints: checkpoint PATCH/DELETE, `GET /exports/:archiveId` download, metrics endpoint.
6. **`structure.md`** — Missing files: `quality.controller.ts`, `quality.service.ts`, `quality.routes.ts`, `audit.controller.ts`, `audit.routes.ts`, `property.model.ts`. Migration list (001-008) covers only 8 tables but data model has 20+.
7. **`design.md` tech stack** — Missing `archiver` (for ZIP export).
8. **No questions about** registration default role, whether `hotel_admin` can be self-registered, rate limiting specifics, or what happens to group data on account deletion.

On duplicate key with same request hash, return stored response; mismatched hash returns 409. Avoid storing update idempotency directly on `itinerary_items` rows.", "new_string": "**Solution:** Use a dedicated `idempotency_keys` table (`key`, `actor_id`, `operation`, `resource_scope`, `request_hash`, `response_snapshot`, `expires_at`). On duplicate key with same request hash, return stored response; mismatched hash returns 409. The `idempotency_key` column on `itinerary_items` is retained for create-only dedup (UNIQUE constraint catches duplicate inserts), while the `idempotency_keys` table handles update idempotency with response replay.

---

## 18. Registration Default Role — What Role Does a New User Get?

**Question:** The prompt defines four roles (hotel_admin, manager, analyst, member) but doesn't specify what role a self-registered user receives.

**Assumption:** New users register with the `member` role by default. Only a `hotel_admin` can promote users to other roles. The first user in the system is seeded as `hotel_admin` — self-registration never grants admin/manager/analyst.

**Solution:** `POST /auth/register` always sets `role = 'member'`. Add `PATCH /accounts/:userId/role` (hotel_admin only) for role assignment. Seeder creates the initial admin user.

---

## 19. Rate Limiting — What Are the Limits?

**Question:** The architecture mentions rate-limit middleware but the prompt doesn't specify thresholds.

**Assumption:** Per-IP rate limit: 100 requests/minute for general endpoints, 10 requests/minute for auth endpoints (login/register) to mitigate brute force. Per-user rate limit: 200 requests/minute.

**Solution:** `rate-limit.middleware.ts` using `express-rate-limit` with separate limiters for auth and general routes. Returns 429 with `Retry-After` header when exceeded.

---

## 20. Account Deletion — What Happens to Group Data?

**Question:** When a user soft-deletes their account, what happens to groups they own, their group memberships, files they uploaded, and itinerary items they created?

**Assumption:** Soft-deleted users are removed from all groups. If the deleted user is a group owner, ownership transfers to the next admin, or if none exists, the group is archived. Files and itinerary items created by the user remain (attributed to a "deleted user" placeholder). Face enrollments are deactivated.

**Solution:** Account deletion triggers a cascade: deactivate face enrollments, remove group memberships, transfer or archive owned groups, and log the full operation in audit_logs. The user row retains `deleted_at` for data retention.

---

## 21. SHA-256 Deduplication — Global Unique Constraint vs Multi-Group Files

**Question:** Q7 assumed global dedup with a unique index on `files.sha256`, but the `files` table has `group_id` directly. If the same file is uploaded to two groups, a unique SHA-256 constraint would block the second upload.

**Assumption:** Deduplication is scoped per-group (UNIQUE on `sha256 + group_id`), not global. This avoids cross-group information leakage and allows the same file content in multiple groups without a join table.

**Solution:** Change the unique constraint to `UNIQUE (sha256, group_id)` on the `files` table. On upload, check if the same hash already exists in the same group — if so, return the existing file record. Different groups can independently store files with the same content.

---

## 22. Face Enrollment Sessions — Missing Data Model

**Question:** The face enrollment flow is multi-step (start → capture × 3 → complete), but the data model only has `face_enrollments` (the final result). Where is in-progress session state stored?

**Assumption:** A `face_enrollment_sessions` table tracks the in-progress enrollment, including which angles have been captured, liveness results per angle, and session expiry.

**Solution:** Add `face_enrollment_sessions` table:
```
face_enrollment_sessions
  id              varchar(36) PK
  user_id         varchar(36) FK users
  status          enum DEFAULT in_progress  -- in_progress | completed | expired
  angles_captured json DEFAULT '{}'         -- {left: {captured: bool, liveness: {...}}, ...}
  expires_at      datetime NOT NULL         -- sessions expire after 30 minutes
  created_at      datetime
  updated_at      datetime
```
On `/complete`, the session data is used to build the final `face_enrollments` record. Expired sessions are cleaned up by the face image cleanup job.

---

## 23. Operational Metrics — Missing Data Model

**Question:** The prompt requires "operational metrics for job duration, queue depth, and DB resource usage." The self-test checklist says these should be stored in DB. But there's no table for this in the data model.

**Assumption:** A simple `operational_metrics` table stores timestamped metric snapshots.

**Solution:** Add `operational_metrics` table:
```
operational_metrics
  id              varchar(36) PK
  metric_name     varchar(255) NOT NULL     -- e.g. import_job_duration, face_cleanup_duration
  metric_value    decimal(15,4) NOT NULL
  labels          json                      -- {batchId: ..., jobType: ...}
  trace_id        varchar(36)
  recorded_at     datetime NOT NULL
```
Background jobs record their duration after each run. A periodic job samples DB resource usage (connection pool stats, table sizes).

---

## 24. Export Logs — Missing Explicit Table

**Question:** The prompt says exported CSV/Excel files are "logged with who/when/filters used." Should this be a dedicated table or part of audit_logs?

**Assumption:** Use audit_logs for the audit trail (who/when), but add an `export_records` table to track the actual export files for cleanup and re-download.

**Solution:** Add `export_records` table:
```
export_records
  id              varchar(36) PK
  user_id         varchar(36) FK users
  export_type     varchar(50) NOT NULL      -- report | account_data | audit_log
  format          varchar(10)               -- csv | excel | zip
  filters         json                      -- query parameters used
  file_path       varchar(500) NOT NULL
  created_at      datetime NOT NULL
  expires_at      datetime NOT NULL          -- cleaned up by export cleanup job
```
This covers both report exports (`POST /reports/export`) and account data exports (`POST /accounts/me/export`). The export cleanup job deletes files past `expires_at`.

---

## 25. Notification Cursor — ID-Based vs Composite Cursor

**Question:** design.md section 6 says `cursor = last notification ID seen` using `id > cursor`, but Q12 proposes a composite `{created_at, id}` cursor. UUID v4 IDs are not sortable, so `id > cursor` is unreliable.

**Assumption:** Use the composite cursor approach from Q12 as the canonical design.

**Solution:** Reconcile by updating design.md's notification flow to use the composite cursor: base64-encoded `{created_at, id}`, queried with `(created_at > ts) OR (created_at = ts AND id > id)`. This is stable under concurrent inserts unlike raw UUID comparison.

---

## 26. Checkpoint Update/Delete — Missing API Endpoints

**Question:** Q5 describes checkpoint reordering via PATCH, but the API spec only has GET and POST for checkpoints. No PATCH or DELETE for individual checkpoints.

**Assumption:** Individual checkpoint update (reorder/edit) and delete endpoints are needed.

**Solution:** Add to API spec:
- `PATCH /groups/:groupId/itineraries/:itemId/checkpoints/:checkpointId` — update position, label, description
- `DELETE /groups/:groupId/itineraries/:itemId/checkpoints/:checkpointId` — remove checkpoint

---

## 27. Export Archive Download — Missing API Endpoint

**Question:** Q13 mentions `GET /exports/:archiveId` as the download endpoint, but it's not in the API spec.

**Assumption:** This endpoint is needed for both account data exports and report exports.

**Solution:** Add `GET /exports/:id` to API spec — serves the file referenced by `export_records.file_path`. Requires authentication; only the user who created the export (or hotel_admin) can download it. Returns 404 if expired/cleaned up."}

content.name: Edit

  - name: Health
  - name: Auth
  - name: Accounts
  - name: Groups
  - name: Itineraries
  - name: Files
  - name: Notifications
  - name: Reports
  - name: Import
  - name: Face
  - name: Quality
  - name: Audit", "new_string": "  /exports/{id}:
    get:
      tags: [Exports]
      summary: Download export archive
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            format: uuid
      responses:
        "200":
          description: File download
        "404":
          description: Export not found or expired
        "403":
          description: Not your export

  /accounts/{userId}/role:
    patch:
      tags: [Accounts]
      summary: Assign role to user (Hotel Admin only)
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: userId
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            format: uuid
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [role]
              properties:
                role:
                  type: string
                  enum: [hotel_admin, manager, analyst, member]
                propertyId:
                  type: string
                  format: uuid
                  description: Required when role is manager
                piiExportAllowed:
                  type: boolean
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Role updated
        "403":
          description: Not Hotel Admin

  /metrics:
    get:
      tags: [Metrics]
      summary: Get operational metrics (Hotel Admin only)
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: metricName
          schema:
            type: string
        - in: query
          name: from
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date-time
        - in: query
          name: to
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date-time
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Metrics

  - name: Files
  - name: Notifications
  - name: Reports
  - name: Import

  - name: Quality
  - name: Audit
  - name: Exports
  - name: Metrics"}

Done. Here's a summary of everything I found and fixed:

### New Questions Added (Q18–Q27 in `questions.md`)
| # | Topic | Decision |
|---|-------|----------|
| Q18 | Registration default role | `member` by default, only admin promotes |
| Q19 | Rate limiting thresholds | 10/min auth, 100/min general, 200/min per-user |
| Q20 | Account deletion cascade | Transfer group ownership, deactivate face enrollments, remove memberships |
| Q21 | SHA-256 dedup scope | Per-group (`UNIQUE(sha256, group_id)`), not global — avoids cross-group leakage |
| Q22 | Face enrollment sessions | New `face_enrollment_sessions` table with 30-min expiry |
| Q23 | Operational metrics | New `operational_metrics` table for job durations, DB stats |
| Q24 | Export logs | New `export_records` table with expiry for cleanup |
| Q25 | Notification cursor | Reconciled to composite `{created_at, id}` cursor (UUID not sortable) |
| Q26 | Checkpoint update/delete | Added missing PATCH/DELETE endpoints for individual checkpoints |
| Q27 | Export download | Added `GET /exports/:id` endpoint |

### Data Model Fixes (`design.md`)
- Added `pii_export_allowed` column to `users`
- Added `meetup_sort_at` derived column to `itinerary_items`
- Added `UNIQUE(sha256, group_id)` constraint on `files`
- Added 4 new tables: `face_enrollment_sessions`, `idempotency_keys`, `export_records`, `operational_metrics`
- Fixed notification cursor flow to composite approach
- Added `archiver` to tech stack
- Added 3 new background jobs (idempotency cleanup, session expiry, enrollment session expiry)
- Added indexes for new tables

### API Spec Fixes (`api-spec.md`)
- Added `PATCH/DELETE` for individual checkpoints
- Added `GET /exports/:id` download endpoint
- Added `PATCH /accounts/:userId/role` (admin role assignment)
- Added `GET /metrics` operational metrics endpoint
- Added `Exports` and `Metrics` tags

### Structure Fixes (`structure.md`)
- Added 6 missing models: `property`, `import`, `quality`, `metric`, `export`, updated `face`
- Added 4 missing controllers: `quality`, `audit`, `exports`
- Added 3 missing services: `quality`, `metric`, `export`
- Added 3 missing route files: `quality`, `audit`, `exports`
- Expanded migrations from 8 to 14, covering all 20+ tables with comments

### Other Doc Updates
- `features.md` — added default role, role assignment endpoint, metrics endpoint, export download, session tracking
- `AI-self-test.md` — added checklist items for new features, background jobs, rate limiting
